file_insights.content: Added CI workflow badge after title using standard GitHub Actions badge.svg syntax: `![CI](https://github.com/ryanmaclean/bop/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)`
file_insights.formatting: Badge placed with blank line before and after for proper markdown spacing

patterns_discovered.usage: Standard pattern for CI status visualization in documentation

patterns_discovered.placement: Title → blank line → badge → blank line → content
patterns_discovered.observation: Maintains consistent markdown formatting and readability

patterns_discovered.source: Memory context indicates this is the canonical upstream repository

gotchas_discovered.description: CI workflow badge will show 'unknown' status until workflow actually runs, which may confuse users viewing the repository before first CI execution

approach_outcome.task_completion: Task completed in first attempt without iteration
approach_outcome.implementation_quality: Simple, correct, and follows markdown/GitHub conventions
approach_outcome.commit_quality: Properly prefixed commit message: 'auto-claude: subtask-3-1 - Add CI workflow badge to README.md after title'

recommendations.recommendation: Verify badge renders and updates correctly
recommendations.action: Confirm badge appears in GitHub web UI after merge and updates when CI runs
If additional badges needed (coverage, license, version), establish consistent placement and styling convention
Ensure .github/workflows/ci.yml exists and runs successfully to activate badge status display
